unit: Migrate ev-loop-interactive + ev-run (+ ev-loop-confidence) to loom parse-plan

contract.goal: Migrate the ev skill bodies off ad-hoc PLAN.md prose-parsing onto the loom parse-plan verb (Unit 3), completing the Phase 1 exit criterion 'no skill body re-implements PLAN.md parsing'. Bake in the decompose-at-runtime model: the loop derives deliverables from a phase's goal + exitCriteria, not a PLAN.md-enumerated list.
acceptance_criteria: ev-loop-interactive Step 1: derive deliverables at runtime via loom parse-plan (phase goal + exitCriteria); explicit no-prose-parse
ev-loop-interactive § Ordering: reframed for decompose-at-runtime (loop decomposes + orders)
ev-loop-interactive § Whiteboard: override from loom parse-plan whiteboard field (phase over plan); DSL parse stays in the skill
ev-run § 3: phase dependencies from loom parse-plan phasesById[n].dependsOn, satisfied when phase manifest status is completed
ev-loop-confidence § Whiteboard: same override migration (consistency with the exit criterion)
No skill body parses PLAN.md prose for the migrated concerns (phases/deps/deliverables/whiteboard)
rules_applied: Skills shell to the verb (markdown prose can't import the lib)
Decompose-at-runtime: PLAN.md gives goal + exitCriteria; the loop decomposes units
The whiteboard DSL parse stays in the skill — the verb only hands off the raw block string (Unit 1 design)
Out-of-scope reads left untouched: ev-run per-phase loop override + candidate-phase descriptions; ev-loop-confidence loose phase-description read (parsePlan does not model these)
disqualifiers: leaving a PLAN.md-prose parse for a migrated concern
moving the whiteboard DSL parse into the verb

actions: ev-loop-interactive: Step 1 now runs loom parse-plan and decomposes deliverables at runtime from goal + exitCriteria (explicit no-prose-parse); § Ordering reframed around the runtime decomposition; § Whiteboard override reads the verb's whiteboard field (phase over plan), DSL parse retained in the skill
ev-run: § 3 reads phase dependencies from loom parse-plan phasesById[n].dependsOn, satisfied when the phase manifest status is completed
ev-loop-confidence: § Whiteboard override migrated to the verb (included to honor 'no skill re-implements parsing' across all three loops)

Verified: grep confirms no residual prose-parsing for migrated concerns (only the new negative instruction matches); loom parse-plan referenced in all three skills; full loom suite 168/168 (unaffected — ev markdown only)

changes_since_previous: Final unit of Phase 1. Routes all three ev loops' PLAN.md structure-reads through the Unit 3 loom parse-plan verb, completing the 'no skill re-implements PLAN.md parsing' exit criterion and baking in decompose-at-runtime.
notes_for_pr: Phase 1 exit criteria satisfied AS WRITTEN — reconsidered the earlier 'PLAN.md prose is stale' note: the three exit criteria (typed parsePlan returning phases/exitCriteria/dependencies; unit-tested against real fixtures; no skill re-implements parsing) all hold. Decompose-at-runtime was an implementation choice within them, and consumer migration IS the 'instead of re-deriving in skill prose' the goal calls for. No PLAN.md revision warranted.
Scope-shift detection: phase-boundary signal fired (last unit) but no second signal concurred — single signal, no revise offered.

Installed-vs-source: this migration + the Unit 3 verb become RUNTIME-active only after the loom + ev plugins are re-installed. The source is correct; the running session uses the stale installed copies.

Out-of-scope (left as-is, parsePlan does not model them): ev-run per-phase loop override + auto-mode candidate-phase PLAN.md descriptions; ev-loop-confidence loose 'phase description in PLAN.md or config.json' read. Candidate follow-up if a future unit wants those modeled.
ev-loop-confidence was added to this unit's scope (beyond the named ev-loop-interactive + ev-run) to honor the exit criterion consistently — it shares the same **Whiteboard** block read.
#70 lifecycle (pr-opened + pr-merged) reconciled in this commit.
